references.text: Barlow, BA. 1997. Viscaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 13: 403-442
Danser, BH. 1935. Revision of the Philippine Loranthaceae. Philippine Journal of Science 58: 128-151.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 8 May 2025

species.citation: (Merr.) Danser , PJS 58 (1935) 132 ; Barlow, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 413; ≡ Ginalloa cumingiana var. angustifolia Merr. , PJS 4c (1909) 153; Type: BS 1139 Banks, Negros.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BUCAS GRANDE (photos), LUZON: Apayao (photos), Aurora (photos), Benguet, Ifugao, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon (photos), NEGROS, PALAWAN.

species.citation: Korth. , Verh. Batav. Genootsch. Kunst. 17 (1839) 260 ; Danser, PJS 58 (1935) 130; Barlow, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 414; Type: from Kalimantan, Borneo. = Ginalloa cumingiana (C.Presl) Benth. & Hook.f. ex Fern.-Vill. , Fl. Filip., 3rd Ed. 4(Nov. App.) (1880) 185. = Ginalloa lanceolata C.B.Rob. , PJS 6c (1911) 200.
species.distribution: Borneo, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi. BOHOL, CATANDUANES, LUZON: Apayao, Benguet, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, ?Bukidnon (photos), NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), POLILLO.
species.notes: Primary and secondary humid forests and disturbed sites. Recorded hosts: Citrus, Mallotus, Mitrephora, Parinari, Schima, Shorea & Syzygium. 0-2100m.

species.citation: Craib , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1911 (1911) 455 ; Danser, Bull. Jard. Bot. Buitenz. 3, 16 (1938) 47; Barlow, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 417; Type: from Thailand. = Ginalloa ovata Danser , PJS 58 (1935) 133; Type: FB 30844 Oro, Luzon.
species.distribution: Cambodia, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Thailand. LUZON: Quezon.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 23 November 2021

species.citation: (Thunb.) Engl. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. Nachtr. 1 (1897) 138 ; Barlow, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 421; ≡ Viscum japonicum Thunb. , Trans. Linn. Soc. London, 1st Ser. 2 (1794) 329; Type: from Japan. = Korthalsella opuntia (Thunb.) Merr. , Bot. Mag. (Tokyo) 30 (1916) 68, nom. illeg. ; Danser, PJS 58 (1935) 134; ≡ Bifaria opuntia (Thunb.) Merr. , EPFP 2 (1923) 113; ≡ Viscum opuntia Thunb. , Fl. Jap. (Thunberg) (1784) 64, nom. illeg., nom. superfl.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, China, India, Japan, Java, Korea, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Pacific Ocean, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. LUZON: Aurora (photos), Benguet, Cagayan (photos), Ifugao, Ilocos Norte (photos), Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Zambales (photos), NEGROS.
species.notes: Mossy forests and ericoid brushwood. Recorded hosts: Diplycosia, Eurya, Rhododendron, Symplocos, Ternstroemia, Vaccinium. 1400-2700m.

The photos by L.L.Co suggest Korthalsella papuana, however, this species typically has obovate internodes that are tapering at the base. This mistletoe has broad internode bases, so it may be a new species.

species.citation: (Thwaites) Oliv. , J. Proc. Linn. Soc., Bot. 7 (1863) 104 ; Hook.f., Fl. Brit. India 5 (1885) 227; Barlow, Brunonia 6 (1983) 19; Barlow, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 428; ≡ Viscum floccosum Thwaites , Enum. Pl. Zeyl. (1864) 418; Type: from Sri Lanka. = Notothixos curranii Merr. , PJS 4c (1909) 152 ; Danser, PJS 58 (1935) 136; Type: FB 12402 Merritt & Curran, Luzon: Bataan, Mt Limay.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Sri Lanka. LUZON: Bataan, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, SIBUYAN.

species.citation: K.Schum. , Nachtr. Fl. Schutzgeb. Südsee (1905) 260 ; Danser, Bull. Jard. Bot. Buitenz. 3, 11 (1931) 456; Danser, PJS 58 (1935) 138; Barlow, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 428; Type: from New Britain. = Notothixos philippinensis Elmer , LPB 2 (1908) 471, as 'philippinense'; Type: Elmer 10114, Negros: Negros Oriental, Cuernos de Negros Mtns, May-1908.
species.distribution: Australia, Lesser Sunda Isls, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls. MINDANAO: Zamboanga del Sur (photos), NEGROS.
species.notes: Primary and secondary summit forests and cultivated lands. Recorded hosts: Aegle, Citrus, Decaisnina, Dendrophthoe, Elaeocarpus, Gironniera, Haplolobus, Macaranga, Pangium, Pometia, Prunus. 0-1200m.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 4c (1909) 152 ; Danser, PJS 58 (1935) 137; Barlow, Brunonia 6 (1983) 22; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 431; Type: FB 9132 Whitford & Hutchinson, Mindanao: Zamboanga.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines, Sulawesi. MINDANAO: Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 13 October 2024

species.citation: Burm.f. , Fl. Ind. (N. L. Burman) (1768) 311 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 113; Danser, Bull. Jard. Bot. Buitenz. 3, 11 (1931) 460; Danser, PJS 58 (1935) 141; Blumea 4 (1941) 280; Barlow, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 433; Type: from Java. = Viscum angulatum B.Heyne ex DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 4 (1830) 283 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 113.
species.distribution: Australia, India, Philippines, Vietnam. CEBU (photos), LUZON: Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Kalinga (photos), La Union, Laguna, NCR, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), SIBUYAN (photos).
species.notes: Open or closed forests. Usually parasitic on Loranthaceae and other Viscaceae. 0-1500m.

species.citation: Elmer , LPB 8 (1919) 3089 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 113; Danser, Bull. Jard. Bot. Buitenz. 3, 11 (1931) 464; Danser, PJS 58 (1935) 142; Blumea 4 (1941) 295; Barlow, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 437; Type: Elmer 17777, Luzon: Laguna, Mt Makiling.
species.distribution: China, Philippines, Sumatra. LUZON: Apayao (photos), Aurora, Laguna.
species.notes: Recorded hosts are four closely related genera of Loranthaceae (Amyema (photos), Dendrophthoe, Scurrula, Taxillus). 900-1800(-2650)m.

species.citation: Wall. ex DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 4 (1830) 278 ; Danser, Bull. Jard. Bot. Buitenz. 3, 16 (1938) 460; Barlow, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 13 (1997) 439; Type: from Penang, Malaysia. ≠ Viscum orientale auct. non Willd. ; Merr., EPFP 2 (1923) 113; Danser, Bull. Jard. Bot. Buitenz. 3, 11 (1931) 466; PJS 58 (1935) 139.
species.distribution: Australia, Hong Kong, Myanmar, Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, MINDANAO: Bukidnon (photos), PALAWAN.
species.notes: In mangroves and open and closed forests. Recorded commonly on Ficus, but also on many other hosts. 0-500(-1400)m.
